item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But do they like Jazz?

+1

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Sounds good to me!

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Orchestra bees is always a favorite. :) With respect to potential crosslinks, the bees attempting to do human things here reminds me of SCP-2849, and the similarity in behavior between the two phenomenon wouldn't likely go unnoticed by the SCPF. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-2339 was first discovered during a building inspection before the scheduled demolition of the abandoned ██████ Concert Hall, ██████. The nest was found to be present in the main auditorium of the building, and reported by the inspector due to its size. The Foundation learned of this through an informant in the local council and sent an agent to investigate, who discovered the anomaly during a performance.

To this:

SCP-2339 was first discovered during a building inspection before the scheduled demolition of the abandoned ██████ Concert Hall, ██████. The nest was found to be present in the main auditorium of the building, and reported by the inspector due to its size. The Foundation learned of this through an informant in the local council and sent an agent to investigate, who discovered the anomaly during a performance. Similarities between the anomalous phenomenon observed in SCP-2339 and that of SCP-2849 have been noted. An investigation into a potentially shared origin is ongoing.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!
Just noticed this has long-since broken the the triple-digit threshold! It's well-deserved, 'tis the bee's knees.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is official Lily-sanctioned fanart. Thanks War.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ The beest fanart ever
- gfdi

lily asked me to put this here

Good scip tho, would updoot again +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doesn't elicit anything from me. The notion of bees that can play musical instruments is neither cute (not that I'm particularly sensitive towards that) nor weird. Sure, it's a strange premise on the surface, but only insofar as concerning the melding-together of two disparate elements in the same vein as the lolrandom premises that form the basis of a bunch of other articles on the wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This passed all the usual SPaG stuff, and its friggin cute to boot.
But now I'm imagining bees trying too sing by buzzing and I think my soul itself it melting. Help. Imagine the little buzz-doots. doot doot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't you mean those pretentious creator-wannbees?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There isn't really that much to this, but what there is is really cute. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, C-Dives translated this into Japanese, found here!

Go take a look if you understand Japanese, and thank you to C-Dives!

item.title: By a strange coincidence...
item.content:encoded:
Heard about this art installation a few days ago:

http://www.itsnicethat.com/news/wolfgang-buttress-hive-expo-pavilion-in-kew-170616

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty much this ^

I liked it, if just for the charming quirkiness. And BEEEEES.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
According to all known laws of musical theory, there is no way bees should be able to play in an orchestra.

Of course, bees do so anyway because they don't care what humans think.

I really like this one because it's a short, snappy read, and it gets you straight to the point without any fuss. We have a lot of long thorough SCPs, so short ones are kind of a dying art, yet they are often so effective. This one works because it's a fucking bee orchestra. That's it. It doesn't strive to be anything more than it is, which is quick and entertaining.

It's one of those skips that makes you think it should be a -J, but then you say 'ah, what the heck,' because it's just so good. I definitely enjoyed it.

+1 Musical bees

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is amusing, but it doesn't really go anywhere as a narrative, and doesn't really show a depth of contextual richness. To be honest, I'd be willing to go along with the lack of a narrative if there was a more rounded and thorough description of the relationship these things have to music.

Articles that play on the real world and which pay close attention to real history and culture are some of my favourites, and can actually barrel through a lot of problems which would sink lesser articles. Smapti's SCP-1529 is a great example - it's a monster that kills you by [DATA EXPUNGED], which would normally sink the article, but the amount of attention paid to the world of mountaineering and the peculiar history and culture of people who attempt Everest smooths over everything. There are other examples (Metaphysician is one writer who pays a lot of attention to context in a way that really benefits his work), but the other article which springs to mind here is PeppersGhost's SCP-1921 - PeppersGhost could easily have not included the specifics of the calliope playlist and it wouldn't really have harmed the article, but the main thing I remember about it nowadays is that they went out of their way to use real, obscure calliope tracks, thereby giving their article a lot of flavour which would otherwise be missing.

You don't have to do this - the article is doing fine anyway - but I feel like if this actually dealt with classical music in an involved way that demonstrated depth of understanding, I'd really like it. If these bees actually had a definable musical taste or preferences, or use a particular repertoire which wasn't a joke, I'd be totally down for this. Maybe they have a thing for some obscure composer and favour his or her works, maybe they refuse to learn certain pieces, maybe they consistently screw up a particular composition but keep playing it anyway. Just something to indicate that these bees interact with the tradition of classical music in a way that gives the piece character and fits it into the real world.

item.title: Minor stylistic comment
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2339 is the collective designation for an anomalously large Bombus terrestris (buff-tailed bumblebee) nest and the bees residing within

I first misread this as saying the bees themselves were anomalously large. It might be less clunky to write something like:

SCP-2339 is an anomalously large nest of bees, of genus Bombus terrestris (buff-tailed bumblebee)

85000

I suggest inserting a comma for readability: 85,000

usually of the classical genre.

"The" classical genre? You could say "of classical genres", or "western orchestral music written 16th-19th century", or something that suggests the Foundation documenter knows about the topic.

Flight of the Bumblebee

Do they prefer to play in the key of B?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this! It's a relatively simple idea, but well-presented. The relative shortness does it favours - if this had 87 exploration logs, incident reports and addendums the joke would be stretched too far, but I think the brevity really works here. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not supposed to be AWCY, no

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ]Im thinking the person who posted it made as a Joke. I personally cannot see any ties to AWCY within the SCP. give lily their upvoteback plz

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If AWCY? is linked to this at any point, I'm taking away my upvote. Those pretentious creator-wannabes have had way too much exposure already.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess someone from AWCY made this after reading Worm?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While this seems perfectly fine and actually Quite better than anything I could write TBH.

It just seems like a normal bland "Magical Animals" type skip. While the Whole Bee orchestra is such a nice touch. I think there should probabbly be more to it , Like How did the bees learn to play music ?, Why ?.

But the Idea is Charming Enough to Upvote. Verdict….Novote …ergh I feel like this is just too cute… Upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Given that you specify later that the queen and 100 drones act as conductor, you should probably take out the second part of this.

Of 85,000 bees, at any given time during a performance 42,500 are performing of which about 100 are assisting the queen with the baton, while the remaining bees rest. I don't see any contradiction here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This isn't bad, but them playing Flight of the Bumblebee is really on the nose.

Around half of the bees play the instruments or act as the conductor,

Given that you specify later that the queen and 100 drones act as conductor, you should probably take out the second part of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a neat idea, and technically well written, but there's nothing about it that particularly captures my attention. I think that you could have explored this further, so I'm also neutral voting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because someone has to say it: What if an orchestra was bees?

Neutral voting for now. It's cute and weird, but is it cute and weird enough for that alone to carry it? Need to mull on this one for a while.

Edit
Final verdict: Eh, what the hell. It's weird and cute, and I keep thinking about it.

+1 for the orchestra that was bees.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There isn't much to this, just some bumblebees that play Flight of the Bumblebee. There's nothing to dislike here either, though. Neutral voting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this.

Its a simple premise, but one with great imagery and is a fun and fast read. Is including "Flight of the Bumblebee" cliche? Probably. Is it any less adorable here? Nope.

+1 Lily. This is a solid SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First Scip! Yay!

Thanks to Tuomey, Lex1nat0r, Soundchaser, ettaHdaM, and Bunton in chat for reviewing. Special thanks to Jekeled for helping me come up with the numbers.

Enjoy!

Edit: Apparently if you flip the number upside down it says bees so… wow, that's a weird coincidence.
